items.message: Thank you for your feedback. Tagging and routing to the team member(s) best able to assist.

items.body:
## đźŽŻ Agentic Issue Triage

**Summary:** This bot-filed issue identifies documentation gaps in `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` introduced by PR #58252, covering chat isolation key enforcement, metadata constraints, response ID validation, `FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy`, breaking error shape changes, and OpenTelemetry instrumentation.

<details>
<summary>đź“‹ Issue Details</summary>

**Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` (beta.2)
**Affected API:** README.md â€” Key concepts, Logging, Troubleshooting sections
**Scenarios:**
- Users encountering 404s when reusing a response without the correct `x-agent-chat-isolation-key`
- Users hitting 400 errors due to undocumented metadata map constraints (16 keys, 64-char keys, 512-char values)
- Integrators passing non-conforming response IDs and receiving 400 errors without guidance
- Operators missing `FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy` observability in production deployments
- Users upgrading from beta.1 surprised by 400â†’404 error shape changes

**Root ask:** Update `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md` with new subsections (Chat Isolation, Metadata constraints, Input validation), a revised Logging section mentioning `FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy` and `AddHttpClientInstrumentation`, and updated Common errors to reflect beta.2 breaking changes.
</details>

<details>
<summary>đź”Ž Debugging / Reproduction Notes</summary>

This is a documentation-only issue; no code defect is present. The implementation guide in the issue body specifies exact markdown changes to the README.

**Suggested investigation steps:**
1. Review PR #58252 changes to confirm the behavioral changes described (chat isolation key enforcement, metadata limits, ID format validation)
2. Apply the README edits described in the Implementation Guide section of the issue
3. Run `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 agentserver` to confirm no snippet placeholders break
4. Run `dotnet build sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/` to ensure no build regressions
</details>

<details>
<summary>đźŹ·ď¸Ź Label Confidence</summary>

- **Category:** `Client` â€” `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` is a data plane (non-management, non-provisioning) SDK under `/sdk/agentserver/`
- **Service:** `AI Agents` â€” CODEOWNERS maps `/sdk/agentserver/` to `%AI Agents`; the label exists in the repository with color #e99695 (service label)
- **Confidence:** High â€” the issue explicitly names the package and service directory; no ambiguity between services or categories
</details>

<details>
<summary>đź‘Ą Owner Routing</summary>

- **Matched CODEOWNERS entry:** `# ServiceLabel: %AI Agents` (line 85) â€” only label required is "AI Agents", which is present in the issue's predicted labels
- **AzureSdkOwners:** none listed
- **ServiceOwners:** `@ankitbko`, `@RaviPidaparthi`
- **Routing action:** Added "Service Attention" label; issue left unassigned (ServiceOwners path, no AzureSdkOwners). "needs-team-attention" not added because the issue is not customer-reported (filed by bot).
- **Scan notes:** Scanned bottom-to-top; management-specific entries (lines 912+) all require `%Mgmt` which this issue does not have; `%AI Model Inference` entry (line 94) requires that label, not present; `%AI Agents` (line 85) matched first with no conflicting multi-label entries.
</details>
